summary: The current evidence set contains no current multi-process distributed performance or fault row that can support successor or comparator-performance wording.

rows.scope: multi-node or multi-process distributed throughput and latency
rows.current_evidence: not present in the registered artifact set
rows.reason: No current run binds topology, transport, media, workload, and attribution to a distributed TideFS storage mode.

rows.scope: distributed sync write, fsync, flush, or durability-latency behavior
rows.current_evidence: not present in the registered artifact set
rows.reason: No current run proves durability-visible distributed behavior or separates client, transport, quorum, and media cost.

rows.scope: degraded read/write service during node, process, network, or device fault
rows.current_evidence: not present in the registered artifact set
rows.reason: No current run proves degraded-visible client behavior or operator-visible refusal for a named distributed topology.

rows.scope: repair, rebuild, re-replication, scrub, and recovery after distributed faults
rows.current_evidence: not present in the registered artifact set
rows.reason: No current run proves recovery completion, data availability, consistency, or performance under a named fault model.

rows.scope: TCP, RDMA, loopback, or mixed transport behavior
rows.current_evidence: not present in the registered artifact set
rows.reason: No current run ties measurements or fault effects to a named transport mode.

rows.scope: OpenZFS, Ceph, DRBD, or clustered-storage comparison
rows.current_evidence: not present in the registered artifact set
rows.reason: No current run provides a comparable incumbent setup, media class, workload class, measurement attribution, and failure envelope.

claim_boundary: These refusal rows are evidence that the registered artifact was reviewed. They are not product admission, runtime proof, release readiness, or permission to use distributed successor/comparator wording.
